L1.area: VPN client authentication and session handling

L2.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the experimental plugin mode where VPN clients that do not support web-based single sign-on are incorrectly granted full network access. When a client is denied authentication, the plugin returns a synchronous success status code to the VPN server instead of an error or deferred status. Because the VPN server only consults the authentication control file when the plugin defers, the synchronous success return is interpreted as immediate approval, admitting the unauthenticated client to the internal network.

L3.area: VPN client authentication and session handling
L3.description: An authentication bypass vulnerability exists in the experimental plugin mode where VPN clients that do not support web-based single sign-on are incorrectly granted full network access. When a client is denied authentication, the plugin returns a synchronous success status code to the VPN server instead of an error or deferred status. Because the VPN server only consults the authentication control file when the plugin defers, the synchronous success return is interpreted as immediate approval, admitting the unauthenticated client to the internal network.

ground_truth.reason: The plugin's client-deny handler returns a success status code instead of an error, causing the VPN server to treat denied clients as authenticated and granting them full network access without completing OIDC authentication.
